chapter_title.ar: باب مِقْدَارِ الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُجْزِئُ لِلْوُضُوءِ وَ الْغُسْلِ وَ مَنْ تَعَدَّى فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – Measurement of the water which suffices for the Ablution (Wudhu) and the washing, the one who exceeds in the Ablution (Wudhu)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/69)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/69)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ يُجْنِبُ فَيَرْتَمِسُ فِي الْمَاءِ ارْتِمَاسَةً وَاحِدَةً فَيَخْرُجُ يُجْزِئُهُ ذَلِكَ مِنْ غُسْلِهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The man has sexual impurity, so he immerses himself into the water with one immersion, and he comes out, would that suffice him from his washing?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who after sexual relations immerses his whole body in water at once and comes out. ‘Is this sufficient for Ghusl (bath)?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘Yes, it is sufficient.’”
